task_completion.description: Crawl the target website using the SEO Spider tool
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog SEO Spider UI is open with 'https://crawler-test.com/' in the URL bar at the top, and the crawl progress bar or results indicate the crawl has started or completed.

task_completion.description: Identify Meta Refresh redirects in the crawler interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog UI shows a filter or tab specifically selecting 'Meta Refresh' (e.g., under Response Codes or Directives), displaying the identified URLs.

task_completion.description: Identify JavaScript redirects in the crawler interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog UI shows a filter or tab specifically selecting 'JavaScript Redirect' (e.g., under Response Codes or Directives), displaying the identified URLs.

task_completion.description: Export Meta Refresh redirects to the specified CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or terminal showing the file 'meta_refresh_audit.csv' located in the '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Export JavaScript redirects to the specified CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or terminal showing the file 'js_redirect_audit.csv' located in the '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Generate a summary report with counts and technical justification
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of 'redirect_remediation_summary.txt' in '~/Documents/SEO/reports/'. The text must include counts for both Meta Refresh and JavaScript redirects, and a technical justification for remediation (e.g., mentioning conversion to 301 redirects).

integrity.description: The agent used the Screaming Frog GUI to perform the crawl
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Screaming Frog SEO Spider graphical interface being interacted with, rather than just terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The CSV files were exported from the tool, not manually fabricated
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Screaming Frog 'Export' button being used or a 'Save As' dialog originating from the Screaming Frog application, rather than the agent manually typing CSV data into a text editor.

integrity.description: The summary report contains actual counts derived from the crawl
integrity.visual_evidence: The redirect counts written in the summary report should logically match the number of rows/results visible in the Screaming Frog UI for those specific redirect types.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target URL is https://crawler-test.com/. The agent must use Screaming Frog SEO Spider version 23.2 to perform the crawl. The expected export files are ~/Documents/SEO/exports/meta_refresh_audit.csv and ~/Documents/SEO/exports/js_redirect_audit.csv. The summary report must be saved to ~/Documents/SEO/reports/redirect_remediation_summary.txt and must explicitly contain counts for both redirect types and a technical justification for converting them to 301 redirects.
